fields.label: Enabled
fields.description: Route the terminal tool's command execution through a microsandbox microVM instead of running it directly on the host. Falls back to the host when the runtime is unavailable (unsupported platform, missing dependency, or boot failure).

fields.label: Image
fields.description: OCI image the sandbox microVM boots from, e.g. python, alpine, node, ubuntu.

fields.label: CPUs
fields.description: Virtual CPUs allocated to the sandbox.

fields.label: Memory (MiB)
fields.description: Memory allocated to the sandbox, in MiB.

fields.label: Persistent sandbox
fields.description: Reuse one microVM for every command in the session (faster, state/installed packages persist). When off, a fresh microVM boots for every command.

fields.label: Network
fields.description: "public" allows outbound network access from the sandbox; "none" fully isolates it.

fields.label: Idle timeout (s)
fields.description: The runtime stops the microVM after this many seconds without activity, so an abandoned or crashed session doesn't leave it running forever. The next command boots a fresh one.

fields.label: Pre-warm on session start
fields.description: Start booting the microVM in the background as soon as the session starts, instead of waiting for the first terminal command, so that command doesn't pay boot latency.
